summary: Scoped shared contract review found material violations in error envelopes, automation/integration request boundaries, response redaction, signing canonicalization, and bridge token class separation.

findings.invariant: (5) ERROR-ENVELOPE LEAKS: the shared error envelope schema permitting internals (stack traces, raw driver errors, internal paths) to validate as a legal error body.
findings.title: ErrorEnvelope allows arbitrary internal details to validate
findings.recommendation: Replace details: z.record(z.unknown()) with a narrow safe-details union, or strip details from public envelopes except whitelisted validation issue fields.

findings.invariant: (2) OVER-PERMISSIVE SHAPES AT SECURITY BOUNDARIES: z.any()/z.unknown()/.passthrough()/unbounded z.record() in REQUEST schemas that flow into privileged operations (auth, billing, admin, credential handling, automation execution) allowing operator/key smuggling past validation.
findings.title: Automation run inputs can smuggle reserved credential material
findings.recommendation: Validate run inputs against the automation's declared input schema and reject reserved keys such as credentials before execution.

findings.invariant: (2) OVER-PERMISSIVE SHAPES AT SECURITY BOUNDARIES: z.any()/z.unknown()/.passthrough()/unbounded z.record() in REQUEST schemas that flow into privileged operations (auth, billing, admin, credential handling, automation execution) allowing operator/key smuggling past validation.
findings.title: Integration credential config accepts arbitrary key/value bags
findings.recommendation: Bind configValues to the integration definition's credential schema and reject unknown or reserved operator fields before persistence/execution.

findings.invariant: (3) SECRET MATERIAL ON THE WIRE: a RESPONSE schema that permits credential values, decrypted secrets, encryption keys, or token plaintext to be serialized to clients (fields that should be write-only or redacted by contract).
findings.title: Session capture responses can legally serialize captured session state
findings.recommendation: Return only redacted metadata such as status and capturedAt; keep captured cookies/storageState server-side and out of the response contract.

findings.invariant: (3) SECRET MATERIAL ON THE WIRE: a RESPONSE schema that permits credential values, decrypted secrets, encryption keys, or token plaintext to be serialized to clients (fields that should be write-only or redacted by contract).
findings.title: AuthUser passthrough permits password hashes or reset secrets in legal responses
findings.recommendation: Make AuthUser strict or explicitly strip secret-bearing fields in the schema so passwordHash/resetToken-style fields cannot validate in responses.

findings.invariant: (4) SIGNING-BYTES AMBIGUITY: the DelegatedTask canonical signing-byte definition permitting two semantically different tasks to produce identical canonical bytes, or the same task to produce different bytes on the two sides (field-order, optional-field, type-coercion, or unicode ambiguity) - signature malleability or verification skew between Cortex and the daemon.
findings.title: DelegatedTask canonicalization maps non-finite numbers onto JSON null bytes
findings.recommendation: Parse a strict signed-task schema before signing, require finite nonnegative numbers, remove passthrough from signed fields, and throw on non-JSON values instead of JSON.stringify fallback canonicalization.

findings.invariant: (6) CROSS-CLASS TOKEN CONFUSION AT THE CONTRACT: any schema that would let a bridge-token-shaped credential validate where a platform-session credential is expected or vice versa (ch09 §9.2 two-class separation), to the extent the contract encodes it.
findings.title: Bridge provider credential is an unbranded string at the contract boundary
findings.recommendation: Encode bridge credentials as a distinct contract shape or branded token class so a platform-session-shaped token cannot validate as a bridge provider credential.
